channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Court hears asylum seeker filmed alleged rape on Brighton beach in attempt to intervene
item.description: A court in Hove has heard that 20-year-old Egyptian national Karin Al-Danasurt filmed an alleged rape on Brighton beach because he claimed he was attempting to stop the attack. Al-Danasurt is accused ...

item.title: Trump pauses military action against Iran amid Pakistan-mediated ceasefire talks over Strait of Hormuz access
item.description: President Donald Trump has announced a temporary two-week suspension of US military attacks on Iran, contingent upon Tehran agreeing to fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz for safe passage of global shi...

item.title: PDEAs seizes P47.6 million worth of shabu from three suspects in Quezon City buy-bust
item.description: Authorities from the Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency (PDEA) successfully confiscated seven kilograms of shabu, locally known as methamphetamine, with an estimated street value of P47.6 million duri...

item.title: Trump Administration Demands Strait of Hormuz Reopening Amidst Escalating Tensions
item.description: Recent communications from the Trump administration have taken a dramatically assertive and confrontational tone regarding the Strait of Hormuz, a vital waterway for global oil shipments. Reports indi...

item.title: Trump announces temporary ceasefire with Iran over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has repeatedly postponed military action against Iranian infrastructure, marking at least the fourth delay within an 18-day period. The latest development is a temporary ce...

item.title: Donald Trump pauses Iran strikes for negotiations as oil and markets react
item.description: Global financial markets responded sharply after US President Donald Trump announced a two-week suspension of planned strikes on Iran. The pause is tied to talks aimed at reopening the Strait of Hormu...

item.title: South Africa’s agricultural exports hold steady amid Middle East conflict pressures
item.description: As tensions rise in the Middle East due to the ongoing US-Israel conflict with Iran, South Africa’s agricultural trade faces potential risks but remains relatively stable. The Middle East accounts for...

item.title: Rahul Gandhi criticises Trump's aggressive stance on Iran
item.description: Rahul Gandhi, senior leader of the Indian National Congress, expressed strong disapproval of the statements made by former US President Donald Trump regarding the potential military conflict with Iran...

item.title: British charity worker dies after being struck by Kenyan President's speeding motorcade
item.description: Edgar Riches, a 78-year-old British charity worker, tragically died in Nairobi after being struck by a speeding vehicle in a motorcade for Kenyan President William Ruto. The incident occurred on March...

item.title: Sam Smith’s Monitor Engineer Switches to Sennheiser Spectera Wireless System for Residencies in New York and San Francisco
item.description: Sam Smith’s recent high-profile residencies at the Warsaw in Brooklyn and the re-launched Castro Theatre in San Francisco presented significant wireless audio challenges in congested RF environments. ...

item.title: Poll Body's Plans for Bengal: AI Cameras to Prevent Booth Capturing and Cancel Polls if Needed
item.description: The Election Commission of India has outlined a series of new initiatives to ensure free and fair elections in Bengal. Among the key proposals are the deployment of AI-powered cameras to monitor votin...

item.title: President Ramaphosa Launches TRC Housing Assistance Reparations Programme in Ndwedwe
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa is set to officially launch the Truth and Reconciliation Commission (TRC) Housing Assistance Reparations programme today at the Sonkombo Sports Field in Ndwedwe, KwaZulu-Nata...

item.title: James Orengo’s political balancing act with Raila Odinga’s influence
item.description: This article from Nation Media Group explores the intricate political relationship between Siaya Governor James Orengo and the Odinga family, one of the most powerful political dynasties in Kenyan his...

item.title: Reiger Park teens selected for Dubai Mina Cup football tournament
item.description: Three talented teens from Reiger Park, Gauteng, have been selected to compete in the prestigious Mina Cup youth football tournament in Dubai. Brothers Zunaid Ground, 16, and Mikklyn Ground, 15, along ...

item.title: Macklin Celebrini Leads Sharks’ Playoff Push with 107 Points
item.description: Macklin Celebrini, the 19-year-old sensation for the San Jose Sharks, has had a remarkable season, amassing 107 points with 41 goals and 66 assists. His contributions have reignited the Sharks’ playof...

item.title: Spotify Expands Prompted Playlist Feature to Podcasts
item.description: Spotify has expanded its AI-powered Prompted Playlist feature, initially launched for music, to now include podcasts. This tool allows users to generate personalized playlists by providing natural lan...

item.title: Delhi Airport Clarifies After Viral Video of Dog Being Handled
item.description: The Delhi International Airport Limited (DIAL) recently responded to a viral video that claimed a community dog, Dholu, was tied with a rope, dragged, and relocated by airport officials. The video, po...

item.title: House Committee Resumes Investigation into VP Sara Duterte's Impeachment Case
item.description: The House of Representatives Committee on Justice has issued subpoenas to several individuals, including a person identified as 'Mary Grace Piatos,' as it resumes its hearings regarding the impeachmen...

item.title: Alex Eala defeats Julia Grabher to reach Linz Open Round of 16
item.description: Filipina tennis star Alex Eala made an impressive debut at the 2026 Linz Open in Austria, defeating local favorite Julia Grabher in straight sets, 6-4, 6-3, to reach the round of 16. The 20-year-old, ...

item.title: Liberal High-Speed Rail Plan Faces Increased Scrutiny Amid Conflicts of Interest
item.description: The Liberal government's plan for a high-speed rail line in Canada continues to face growing criticism and uncertainty, particularly regarding its viability and the potential conflicts of interest inv...
